1.  Use class-based views (CBVs) if you want the corresponding URLConf to appear in menu.
2.  Use a RedirectView CBV for parents of Dropdown menus. At some places, where even though a URLConf has a childURLConfs, if I am confident that none of the child URLConfs should have a menu-item, I have even used other CBVs. e.g.: The "Pre-formatted Reports" CBV subclasses the TemplateView class.
3.  Instead of using Powershell v5, use Powershell v7. However, do not uninstall Powershell v5 as its required by Windows for some of its operations. \
    To use PowerShell v7, follow these instrustions:
    1.  Download the MSI file from https://github.com/PowerShell/PowerShell/releases or from https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/scripting/install/installing-powershell-on-windows?view=powershell-7.3&WT.mc_id=THOMASMAURER-blog-thmaure#msi
    2.  Install using the downloaded MSI package using an account with admin privileges.
    3.  In visual studio code, perform the following:
        1.  Ctrl+Shift+P "\>Preferences: Open Settings (UI)"
        2.  Search for "Terminal › Integrated › Default Profile: Windows"
        3.  From the dropdown, select the Powershell 7 profile.
        4.  Close existing integrated terminal in VS Code and start a new terminal.
